text:
Once upon an autumn evening in some quaint little town nobody ever visited, there lay a pumpkin patch under a starlit sky. Golden-orange orbs hung ripe like fallen stars. It was the season for Jack-o'-lanterns, but this one was different. It had a nose! A tiny, wiggling nose.

Orwell was the name. Orwell lived there with his eccentric collection of vegetables. They had a special relationship. Orwell would listen to them gossip.

One pumpkin said, "I could almost vote."

Another claimed, "I dream of traveling the world."

Orwell, with his big, curious nose, tilted his head.

"What's so interesting?" Orwell inquired.

"A trip to Neverland would be perfect. Maybe they have chocolate rivers!"

"A river of what?"

"The biggest chocolate stream you ever did see!"

"Oh, I don't know... chocolate rivers don't exist."

After some more back-and-forth about dreams and fantasies, a brave carrot jumped into conversation.
